Parametric Survival models for cTTO data

Canadian Valuation Data

First we set up the data, including stratification (code hidden for readability).data

We first set up some internal functions that will do all the fitting for us (code hidden for readability)

Out of sample predictions, standard 20-dummy functional form

We first use the standard 20-dummy + intercept main effects model, using leave-a-state-out cross-validation to assess model fit.

Now we can look at the fit of the models. To begin, we compare the out-of-sample predicted survival curves for a random sample of 10 health states to the Kaplan-Meier survival curve.

subset <- sample(1:86, 10, replace=FALSE)

all_df2subset <- all_df2 %>% filter(state_id %in% subset)

ggplot(all_df2subset, aes(x = time, y = surv, color = type, linetype=type)) +
  geom_line(aes(color=type,linetype=type),size=0.9,alpha=1) +
  geom_line(data=subset(all_df2subset,type=="KM"),size=1, color="black") +
  geom_line(data=subset(all_df2subset,type=="Tobit"),size=1, color="black") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
 coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 2)) +
  facet_wrap(~ state_id, ncol = 5) +
  # theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
#  scale_color_manual(values = c("KM" = "black", "Stratified Weibull" = "green", "Tobit"="red")) +
  labs(
    title = "Survival Curves by State",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Survival",
    color = "Curve Type",
    linetype="Curve Type"
  ) + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") + 
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position="bottom")
Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_line()`).

Next, we look at the out-of-sample predicted restricted mean survival times, with survival times up to 1, 1.5 and 2. For each model we first plot the predicted restricted mean survival times for the 86 health states vs. the Kaplan-Meier restricted means, the plot the difference between the model-predicted restricted mean survival time and the Kaplan-Meier survival time vs. the Kaplan-Meier survival time (i.e., a Bland-Altman plot). Trends in the plot are modelled via a generalized additive model, and limits of agreement computed using the standard deviation of the points from the fitted mean curve.

ggplot(data=rest_means$RestMean_Diff, aes(y=restricted_mean, x=km_value, color=type)) + 
 geom_point() + 
  facet_grid( type~ tau) + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0,
    slope = 1,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "grey40",
    linewidth = 1
  )+ 
  theme(legend.position="bottom")
Bias_by_TypeandTau <- rest_means$RestMean_Diff %>% group_by(type,tau)%>% summarise(bias = mean(diff_from_KM), sd=sd(diff_from_KM), mae=mean(abs(diff_from_KM)),sd_BA=sd(diff_from_KM - gamfit), .groups="drop")

RestMean_Diff <- 
  rest_means$RestMean_Diff %>% left_join(Bias_by_TypeandTau,by=c("type","tau"))
RestMean_Diff <- RestMean_Diff %>% mutate(loa_lower = gamfit - 1.96*sd_BA, loa_upper = gamfit + 1.96*sd_BA)
RestMean_Diff <- RestMean_Diff %>%
  arrange(type, tau, km_value)

ggplot(data=RestMean_Diff,aes(x=km_value,y=diff_from_KM,color=type,fill=type)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_line(aes(y=gamfit)) +
    facet_grid(type~tau) + 
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50")+
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin=loa_lower, ymax=loa_upper,fill=type,group=interaction(type,tau)),alpha=0.25, color=NA) + 
    labs(
        title = "Regression-Based Bland–Altman Limits of agreement (tau=1, 1.5, 2)",
        x = "Kaplan–Meier Estimate",
        y = "Difference from KM Estimate",
        color = "Model type",
        fill  = "Model type"
    )+ 
  theme(legend.position="bottom")

Summary statistics for the predicted restricted mean survival times (vs. the Kaplan-Meier restricted means) appear below. Ideally we want small absolute bias and the standard deviation of the Bland-Altman limits of agreement (sd_BA) to be as small as possible.

Bias_by_TypeandTau %>% filter(tau==1) %>% kable(digits=3)
type tau bias sd mae sd_BA
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1 1 0.001 0.045 0.034 0.044
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1, 1.5 1 -0.002 0.046 0.035 0.045
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1 1 -0.018 0.051 0.041 0.047
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1, 1.5 1 -0.014 0.050 0.039 0.046
Tobit 1 0.081 0.048 0.085 0.039
Weibull 1 0.003 0.053 0.040 0.050
loglogistic 1 -0.038 0.058 0.051 0.057 
Bias_by_TypeandTau %>% filter(tau==1.5) %>% kable(digits=3)
type tau bias sd mae sd_BA
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1 1.5 0.014 0.055 0.044 0.050
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1, 1.5 1.5 0.011 0.056 0.044 0.052
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1 1.5 -0.002 0.063 0.049 0.062
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1, 1.5 1.5 0.004 0.064 0.049 0.063
Tobit 1.5 0.084 0.054 0.088 0.047
Weibull 1.5 0.015 0.066 0.051 0.066
loglogistic 1.5 -0.021 0.076 0.062 0.074 
Bias_by_TypeandTau %>% filter(tau==2) %>% kable(digits=3)
type tau bias sd mae sd_BA
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1 2 -0.015 0.066 0.051 0.063
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1, 1.5 2 -0.019 0.067 0.053 0.064
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1 2 -0.030 0.081 0.064 0.077
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1, 1.5 2 -0.024 0.082 0.064 0.078
Tobit 2 0.023 0.071 0.057 0.053
Weibull 2 -0.030 0.082 0.066 0.077
loglogistic 2 -0.054 0.097 0.086 0.089

Next we compare predicted medians under cross-validition to Kaplan-Meier medians

# find medians
medians_df <- all_df2 %>% arrange(type,state_id,time) %>% group_by(type,state_id) %>% 
  filter(surv<=0.5) %>% slice(1) %>% 
  ungroup()

km_medians <- medians_df %>% filter(type=="KM") %>% mutate(km_median=time) %>% select(state_id,km_median) %>% arrange(state_id)

medians_df <- medians_df %>% filter(type!="KM") %>% left_join(km_medians,by="state_id") %>% mutate(model_median=time, diff_from_km = model_median-km_median) 

ggplot(medians_df,aes(x=km_median,y=model_median)) + geom_point() + facet_wrap(~type) + geom_abline(slope=1,intercept=0,linetype="dashed") + coord_equal()
ggplot(medians_df,aes(x=km_median,y=diff_from_km)) + geom_point() + facet_wrap(~type) + geom_abline(slope=0,intercept=0,linetype="dashed") 
Median_Diagnostics <- medians_df %>% 
  group_by(type) %>% mutate(diff = (model_median-km_median)) %>%
summarise(MAE=mean(abs(diff)), RMSE=sqrt(mean(diff^2)), bias=mean(diff))

Median_Diagnostics %>% kable(digits=3)
type MAE RMSE bias
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1 0.078 0.099 -0.019
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1, 1.5 0.081 0.101 -0.031
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1 0.073 0.095 -0.050
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1, 1.5 0.070 0.090 -0.036
Tobit 0.078 0.091 0.061
Weibull 0.076 0.097 -0.044
loglogistic 0.092 0.118 -0.054

Sensitivity to censoring point

We fit the models on the full dataset

models_fitted <- fitmodels(data,datasplit2,datasplit,datasplit3,datasplit4,omit.state=100,func.form=~mo + sc + ua + pd + ad,printfit=TRUE)
             df       BIC
mtobit       22 17322.548
mloglogistic 22 10112.836
mweibull     22 10504.899
mweibulli    44  9044.372
mweibullii   66  9159.495
mweibulliii  88  6308.665
mweibulliv   66  6218.612

The coefficients for the non-stratified models

models_fitted$model_table %>%
  kbl(caption = "Fitted Weibull, Log-logistic, and Tobit models", digits=2) %>%
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE, font = "Arial")
Fitted Weibull, Log-logistic, and Tobit models

ua3 0.03 0.03 0.37 0.13 0.03 0.00 0.03 0.02 0.03
ua4 0.16 0.03 0.00 0.30 0.03 0.00 0.13 0.02 0.00
ua5 0.09 0.03 0.00 0.20 0.03 0.00 0.11 0.02 0.00

pd3 0.17 0.03 0.00 0.26 0.03 0.00 0.10 0.02 0.00

ad2 0.06 0.03 0.03 0.09 0.03 0.01 0.05 0.02 0.00
ad3 0.21 0.03 0.00 0.26 0.03 0.00 0.13 0.02 0.00
ad4 0.34 0.03 0.00 0.46 0.03 0.00 0.22 0.02 0.00
ad5 0.36 0.03 0.00 0.46 0.03 0.00 0.25 0.02 0.00

Next we consider censoring disutilities at 1.5 rather than 2 and examine how the predicted mean disutilities change under the various models.

models_fitted_cens1.5 <- fitmodels(data1.5,datasplit2=NULL,datasplit=datasplit1.5,datasplit3=NULL, datasplit4=datasplit41.5,omit.state=100,func.form=~mo + sc + ua + pd + ad,printfit=FALSE)
BIC(models_fitted_cens1.5$mtobit, models_fitted_cens1.5$mweibull, models_fitted_cens1.5$mloglogistic, models_fitted_cens1.5$mweibulli,models_fitted_cens1.5$mweibulliv)
                                   df       BIC
models_fitted_cens1.5$mtobit       22 14975.669
models_fitted_cens1.5$mweibull     22  9608.085
models_fitted_cens1.5$mloglogistic 22  9479.172
models_fitted_cens1.5$mweibulli    44  8381.536
models_fitted_cens1.5$mweibulliv   66  5555.776

Fitted coefficients for the non-stratified models, with censoring at 2 vs. 1.5

ggplot(tbl,
       aes(x = estimate,
           y = reorder(term, estimate),
           colour = censor)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper),
    height = 0.2,
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  labs(
    x = "Coefficient estimate (95% CI)",
    y = NULL,
    colour = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) + labs(
    title = "Caterpillar plot of Weibull AFT/PH estimates"
  ) + 
  facet_wrap(~model)
Warning: `geom_errorbarh()` was deprecated in ggplot2 4.0.0.
ℹ Please use the `orientation` argument of `geom_errorbar()` instead.
`height` was translated to `width`.

Pits (55555) out-of-sample predicted mean disutility under censoring at 2 vs. at 1.5

pitsmeans <- right_join(unrestricted_means,unrestricted_means_1.5,join_by(type)) %>%
  select(type,time.x,time.y,restricted_mean_cens2,restricted_mean_cens1.5) %>%
  rename(time_cens2=time.x,time_cens1.5=time.y)
pitsmeans %>% kable(digits=2)
type time_cens2 time_cens1.5 restricted_mean_cens2 restricted_mean_cens1.5
KM 2.00 1.50 1.03 0.94
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1 2.85 2.11 0.93 0.88
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1, 1.5 3.22 1.51 0.95 0.85
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1 2.85 2.11 1.09 1.00
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1, 1.5 3.22 1.51 1.11 0.95
Tobit 2.17 2.02 0.97 0.98
Weibull 4.50 4.22 1.27 1.23
loglogistic 12.42 12.57 1.81 1.82

The apparent sensitivity to censoring point is an artefact of how I am integrating under the curve (the time points indicate the max time used for the numerical integration). I need to switch to doing the integration analytically, at which point I think the issue will go away.

Functional form used in the Canadian valuation study

We repeat the above, this time using the functional form adopted in the Canadian valuation study

We look at out-of-sample predicted vs. Kaplan-Meier survival curves:

subset <- sample(1:86, 10, replace=FALSE)

all_df2subset <- all_df2_canff %>% filter(state_id %in% subset)

ggplot(all_df2subset, aes(x = time, y = surv, color = type, linetype=type)) +
  geom_line(aes(color=type,linetype=type),size=0.9,alpha=1) +
  geom_line(data=subset(all_df2subset,type=="KM"),size=1, color="black") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
 coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 2)) +
  facet_wrap(~ state_id, ncol = 5) +
  # theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
#  scale_color_manual(values = c("KM" = "black", "Stratified Weibull" = "green", "Tobit"="red")) +
  labs(
    title = "Survival Curves by State",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Survival",
    color = "Curve Type",
    linetype="Curve Type"
  ) + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") + 
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position="bottom")
Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_line()`).

We also compare the out-of-sample predicted restricted mean survival times vs. the Kaplan-Meier

ggplot(data=rest_means_canff$RestMean_Diff, aes(y=restricted_mean, x=km_value, color=type)) + 
 geom_point() + 
  facet_grid( type~ tau) + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0,
    slope = 1,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "grey40",
    linewidth = 1
  )+ 
  theme(legend.position="bottom")
Bias_by_TypeandTau_canff <- rest_means_canff$RestMean_Diff %>% group_by(type,tau)%>% summarise(bias = mean(diff_from_KM), sd=sd(diff_from_KM), mae=mean(abs(diff_from_KM)),sd_BA=sd(diff_from_KM - gamfit), .groups="drop")

RestMean_Diff_canff <- 
  rest_means_canff$RestMean_Diff %>% left_join(Bias_by_TypeandTau,by=c("type","tau"))
RestMean_Diff_canff <- RestMean_Diff_canff %>% mutate(loa_lower = gamfit - 1.96*sd_BA, loa_upper = gamfit + 1.96*sd_BA)
RestMean_Diff_canff <- RestMean_Diff_canff %>%
  arrange(type, tau, km_value)

ggplot(data=RestMean_Diff_canff,aes(x=km_value,y=diff_from_KM,color=type,fill=type)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_line(aes(y=gamfit)) +
    facet_grid(type~tau) + 
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50")+
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin=loa_lower, ymax=loa_upper,fill=type,group=interaction(type,tau)),alpha=0.25, color=NA) + 
    labs(
        title = "Regression-Based Bland–Altman Limits of agreement (tau=1, 1.5, 2)",
        x = "Kaplan–Meier Estimate",
        y = "Difference from KM Estimate",
        color = "Model type",
        fill  = "Model type"
    )+ 
  theme(legend.position="bottom")
Bias_by_TypeandTau_canff %>% filter(tau==1) %>% kable(digits=3)
type tau bias sd mae sd_BA
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1 1 0.005 0.037 0.029 0.030
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1, 1.5 1 0.003 0.038 0.030 0.031
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1 1 -0.013 0.039 0.029 0.029
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1, 1.5 1 -0.009 0.039 0.028 0.029
Tobit 1 0.085 0.039 0.086 0.031
Weibull 1 0.008 0.042 0.034 0.032
loglogistic 1 -0.032 0.043 0.040 0.035 
Bias_by_TypeandTau_canff %>% filter(tau==1.5) %>% kable(digits=3)
type tau bias sd mae sd_BA
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1 1.5 0.019 0.049 0.041 0.037
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1, 1.5 1.5 0.016 0.049 0.040 0.038
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1 1.5 0.005 0.050 0.038 0.038
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1, 1.5 1.5 0.011 0.050 0.039 0.039
Tobit 1.5 0.089 0.045 0.091 0.036
Weibull 1.5 0.022 0.056 0.048 0.042
loglogistic 1.5 -0.014 0.060 0.048 0.046 
Bias_by_TypeandTau_canff %>% filter(tau==2) %>% kable(digits=3)
type tau bias sd mae sd_BA
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1 2 -0.009 0.062 0.047 0.053
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1, 1.5 2 -0.013 0.063 0.049 0.053
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1 2 -0.022 0.066 0.053 0.056
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1, 1.5 2 -0.016 0.066 0.051 0.056
Tobit 2 0.027 0.061 0.051 0.043
Weibull 2 -0.023 0.065 0.051 0.054
loglogistic 2 -0.049 0.073 0.070 0.061

Finally, we compare out-of-sample predicted medians vs. Kaplan-Meier medians

# find medians
medians_df_canff <- all_df2_canff %>% arrange(type,state_id,time) %>% group_by(type,state_id) %>% 
  filter(surv<=0.5) %>% slice(1) %>% 
  ungroup()

km_medians_canff <- medians_df_canff %>% filter(type=="KM") %>% mutate(km_median=time) %>% select(state_id,km_median) %>% arrange(state_id)

medians_df_canff <- medians_df_canff %>% filter(type!="KM") %>% left_join(km_medians,by="state_id") %>% mutate(model_median=time, diff_from_km = model_median-km_median) 

ggplot(medians_df_canff,aes(x=km_median,y=model_median)) + geom_point() + facet_wrap(~type, ncol=3) + geom_abline(slope=1,intercept=0,linetype="dashed") 
ggplot(medians_df_canff,aes(x=km_median,y=diff_from_km)) + geom_point() + facet_wrap(~type, ncol=3) + geom_abline(slope=0,intercept=0,linetype="dashed") 
Median_Diagnostics_canff <- medians_df_canff %>% 
  group_by(type) %>% mutate(diff = (model_median-km_median)) %>%
summarise(MAE=mean(abs(diff)), RMSE=sqrt(mean(diff^2)), bias=mean(diff))

Median_Diagnostics_canff %>% kable(digits=3)
type MAE RMSE bias
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1 0.066 0.083 -0.014
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 0.5, 1, 1.5 0.071 0.086 -0.027
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1 0.060 0.085 -0.043
Stratified Weibull, cutpoints 1, 1.5 0.056 0.079 -0.031
Tobit 0.077 0.089 0.065
Weibull 0.061 0.084 -0.034
loglogistic 0.069 0.090 -0.044

Lastly, compare models on the full dataset

models_canff <- fitmodels(data,datasplit2,datasplit,datasplit3, datasplit4,omit.state=100,func.form=~mo_num + sc_num + ua_num + pd_num + ad_num + mo45 + sc45 + ua45 + ad45 + pd45 + num45sq,printfit=TRUE)
             df       BIC
mtobit       13 17206.747
mloglogistic 13  9859.348
mweibull     13 10337.604
mweibulli    25  8854.106
mweibullii   37  8865.756
mweibulliii  49  6101.471
mweibulliv   37  6100.894
models_canff$model_table %>%
  kbl(caption = "Fitted Weibull, Log-logistic, and Tobit models", digits=2) %>%
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE, font = "Arial")
Fitted Weibull, Log-logistic, and Tobit models
term Weibull AFT/PH_estimate Weibull AFT/PH_std.error Weibull AFT/PH_p.value Log-logistic AFT_estimate Log-logistic AFT_std.error Log-logistic AFT_p.value Tobit AFT_estimate Tobit AFT_std.error Tobit AFT_p.value
(Intercept) -1.40 0.05 0.00 -2.27 0.05 0 -0.10 0.02 0.00
mo_num 0.05 0.01 0.00 0.06 0.01 0 0.03 0.01 0.00
sc_num 0.07 0.01 0.00 0.11 0.01 0 0.04 0.01 0.00
ua_num 0.01 0.01 0.49 0.05 0.01 0 0.01 0.01 0.17
pd_num 0.08 0.01 0.00 0.12 0.01 0 0.05 0.01 0.00
ad_num 0.07 0.01 0.00 0.07 0.01 0 0.05 0.01 0.00
mo45 0.09 0.04 0.02 0.22 0.04 0 0.05 0.02 0.02
sc45 0.08 0.04 0.02 0.16 0.04 0 0.05 0.02 0.02
ua45 0.25 0.04 0.00 0.30 0.04 0 0.13 0.02 0.00
ad45 0.20 0.04 0.00 0.35 0.04 0 0.10 0.02 0.00
pd45 0.15 0.04 0.00 0.24 0.04 0 0.10 0.02 0.00
num45sq -0.04 0.00 0.00 -0.07 0.00 0 -0.01 0.00 0.00
Log(scale) -0.32 0.01 0.00 -0.77 0.01 0 -0.75 0.01 0.00

Outstanding issues:

  • fit a stratified Weibull model with cutpoints at 0.5, 1 so that we can compare censoring at 2 vs. at 1.5

  • the above analyses treat cTTO values from the same individual as independent, i.e., ignore heterogeneity.

  • Furthermore, it treats the probability of any trading as independent of the survival distribution conditional on trading, which is likely not the case. This will lead to bias, as the link functions are not linear. The next step is to fit the logistic model for any trading and the stratified Weibull models using a common (or at least correlated) frailty parameter (i.e., random effect).
